q.en: Who is telling this story, and in what form?
q.zh: 这个故事是谁讲的？以什么形式讲述？

options.en: Hattie MacGruder, writing in her own diary (first person)
options.zh: 海蒂·麦克格罗德尔，写在自己日记里（第一人称）

options.en: Grandma Nettie, telling a bedtime story
options.zh: 内蒂奶奶，在讲睡前故事

options.en: An outside narrator who knows everyone's thoughts
options.zh: 一个了解所有人想法的外部叙述者

options.en: Sarah, writing to prove Hattie was lying
options.zh: 萨拉，写文章证明海蒂在撒谎

explain.en: The book is subtitled 'A story from Hattie MacGruder's diary' and Hattie writes 'My name is Hattie MacGruder' in first person.
explain.zh: 这本书副标题是『海蒂·麦克格罗德尔日记里的故事』，海蒂以第一人称写道：『我叫海蒂·麦克格罗德尔』。

q.en: At the beginning, how did Hattie feel about spending two weeks at Grandma Nettie's?
q.zh: 一开始，海蒂对去内蒂奶奶家待两周有什么感受？

options.en: She didn't want to go and felt two weeks 'might as well be two years'
options.zh: 她根本不想去，觉得两周『简直像两年那么长』

options.en: She was excited and packed right away
options.zh: 她很兴奋，立刻收拾行李

options.en: She was happy to leave her friends behind
options.zh: 她很高兴能甩开朋友们

options.en: She planned to bring Sarah and Sybil along
options.zh: 她打算把萨拉和西比尔一起带上

explain.en: On Diary Day 14 Hattie writes that Mom told her to go for two weeks and thinks 'Two weeks? It might as well be two years... I don't want to go.'
explain.zh: 日记第14天，海蒂写道妈妈让她去两周，她心想『两周？简直像两年……我不想走。』

q.en: What did Hattie find in her grandmother's attic?
q.zh: 海蒂在奶奶的阁楼里发现了什么？

options.en: An old, sweat-stained cowboy hat lying in the middle of the floor
options.zh: 一顶满是汗渍的旧牛仔帽，孤零零躺在阁楼中央

options.en: A chest full of gold coins
options.zh: 一箱金币

options.en: Her grandfather's old computer
options.zh: 她爷爷的旧电脑

options.en: The love note Eric wrote to Sarah
options.zh: 埃里克写给萨拉的情书

explain.en: The attic 'was empty except for an old sweat-stained cowboy hat just lying there in the middle of the floor.' Hattie put it on, and it turned out to be magical.
explain.zh: 阁楼『空荡荡的，只有一顶满是汗渍的旧牛仔帽孤零零躺在地板中央』。海蒂把它戴上，结果它竟然有魔力。

q.en: What happens after Hattie puts on the magical hat?
q.zh: 海蒂戴上那顶有魔力的帽子后，发生了什么？

options.en: She is suddenly transported to a meadow where a man rides up on a golden horse
options.zh: 她忽然被带到了一片草地，一个男人骑着金色骏马来到她面前

options.en: She shrinks to the size of a mouse
options.zh: 她缩小到了老鼠那么小

options.en: She falls asleep and never wakes up
options.zh: 她睡着了，再也没醒

options.en: The attic fills with spiders
options.zh: 阁楼里爬满了蜘蛛

explain.en: Once she puts on the hat, 'suddenly I wasn't in the attic anymore. I was standing in the middle of a grassy meadow' where a man on a gold-colored horse appears.
explain.zh: 她一戴上帽子，『忽然我不在阁楼里了。我站在一片草地中央』，一个骑着金色马的男人出现了。

q.en: From what point of view is Hattie's story written?
q.zh: 海蒂的故事是用什么视角（人称）写的？

options.en: First person—Hattie tells it herself using 'I'
options.zh: 第一人称——由海蒂用『我』自己讲述

options.en: Second person—the reader is 'you'
options.zh: 第二人称——以『你』称呼读者

options.en: Third person—an outside narrator describes her
options.zh: 第三人称——由外部叙述者描写她

options.en: It is written as a script for a play
options.zh: 它被写成了一出剧本

explain.en: Hattie writes in her own voice: 'My name is Hattie MacGruder,' 'I spent two weeks with her,' so it is a first-person diary.
explain.zh: 海蒂用自己的口吻写：『我叫海蒂·麦克格罗德尔』『我和她待了两星期』，所以这是第一人称日记。

q.en: In the 'Special Note,' Hattie says she won't reveal that Eric Ledbetter wrote a love note to Sarah. What does this show about Hattie?
q.zh: 在「特别说明」里，海蒂说她不会透露埃里克·莱德贝特给萨拉写了情书。这说明了海蒂的什么？

options.en: Even though she is mad at Sarah, she is kind enough not to embarrass her
options.zh: 尽管她对萨拉生气，但仍很善良，不愿让萨拉难堪

options.en: She wants to keep the note a secret so she can use it later
options.zh: 她想保密，以便日后利用这封情书

options.en: She forgot what the note said
options.zh: 她忘了情书里写了什么

options.en: She is afraid of Eric Ledbetter
options.zh: 她害怕埃里克·莱德贝特

explain.en: Hattie calls Sarah a liar but writes she won't reveal the note because 'Sarah would be so embarrassed... Even she doesn't deserve that'—showing the author uses this to reveal Hattie's hidden kindness.
explain.zh: 海蒂虽然说萨拉开玩笑，却写道不会公开情书，因为『萨拉会很尴尬……就连她也不该受这种委屈』——作者借此揭示海蒂隐藏的善良。

q.en: Hattie falls asleep reading a book about a girl who pulls the covers over her head when scared. How is Hattie different in the spooky attic?
q.zh: 海蒂看着一本书睡着了，书里有个女孩害怕时会把被子蒙在头上。而在阴森的阁楼里，海蒂有什么不同？

options.en: The attic was just as spooky as the book, but Hattie says she 'wasn't scared, not at all'
options.zh: 阁楼和书里一样阴森，但海蒂说自己『一点都不害怕』

options.en: She also hid under the covers like the book's girl
options.zh: 她也像书里的女孩一样躲进被子里

options.en: She screamed and ran back downstairs
options.zh: 她尖叫着跑下楼

options.en: She called her mom to come get her
options.zh: 她叫妈妈来接她

explain.en: Hattie notes the attic 'was really spooky, just like the book I was reading, but I wasn't scared, not at all,' contrasting herself with the frightened girl in the story.
explain.zh: 海蒂说阁楼『真阴森，就像我正在读的那本书，但我一点都不害怕』，把自己和故事里那个吓坏了的女孩形成对比。

q.en: Who is the man on the golden horse, and how do we know?
q.zh: 骑金色马的男人是谁？我们怎么知道的？

options.en: Hattie's grandfather—Grandma Nettie's husband who loved horses, and the photo's note says 'I love you, Nettie'
options.zh: 海蒂的爷爷——内蒂奶奶爱马、已故的丈夫，照片背面的留言写着『我爱你，内蒂』

options.en: A stranger who just liked Hattie's hat
options.zh: 一个只是喜欢海蒂帽子的陌生人

options.en: Hattie's father
options.zh: 海蒂的父亲

options.en: The town's mayor
options.zh: 镇上的市长

explain.en: Grandma Nettie's husband loved horses and died when Hattie was three; the man gives Hattie a photo with the note 'I love you, Nettie. Always have, always will,' revealing he is her grandfather.
explain.zh: 内蒂奶奶的丈夫爱马，在海蒂三岁时去世；男人给海蒂一张照片，背面写着『我爱你，内蒂。一直都爱，永远都爱』，说明他就是她的爷爷。

q.en: Why does Hattie wear the red bandanna every day after returning home?
q.zh: 回家后，海蒂为什么每天都系着那条红头巾？

options.en: So she will never forget the grandfather she met and the magical experience
options.zh: 这样她就永远不会忘记那位爷爷，以及那段魔法的经历

options.en: Because it matches all her clothes
options.zh: 因为它和她所有衣服都搭

options.en: To show Sarah and Sybil she was right
options.zh: 为了向萨拉和西比尔证明自己没错

options.en: Because her mom made her wear it
options.zh: 因为妈妈逼她戴

explain.en: Hattie writes, 'I wear the bandanna every day so I won't forget,' showing the author wants readers to see how much the memory of her grandfather means to her.
explain.zh: 海蒂写道：『我每天都系着这条头巾，这样就不会忘记。』作者借此让读者看到，爷爷的记忆对她多么珍贵。

q.en: The book's Focus Question asks if Hattie's vacation turned out the way she expected. What is the answer?
q.zh: 书的「聚焦问题」问：海蒂的假期有没有像她预期的那样？答案是什么？

options.en: No—she expected to hate it, but she ended up glad she went and had a magical, meaningful visit
options.zh: 没有——她本以为会讨厌，结果却很高兴去了，还经历了一场神奇而有意义的探访

options.en: Yes—she expected a magical adventure and got exactly that from the start
options.zh: 是的——她一开始就期待魔法冒险，也确实如此

options.en: Yes—she expected to be bored and she was bored
options.zh: 是的——她料到会无聊，也的确无聊

options.en: No—she expected to love it but ended up hating it
options.zh: 没有——她本以为会喜欢，结果却讨厌

explain.en: Hattie opens by saying she 'didn't want to go, but by the time I got back I was glad I had,' and the magical hat visit with her grandfather made the trip unexpectedly special.
explain.zh: 海蒂开头说她『不想去，但回来时却很高兴自己去了』，而和爷爷的那场魔法般相遇，让这趟旅行出奇地特别。

prompt.en: Hattie expected to be miserable at her grandmother's house, but she discovered something magical and learned about her grandfather's love for Grandma Nettie. Think of a time you expected something to be boring or bad but it turned out better than you thought. What changed your mind? How was it like or unlike Hattie's surprise?
prompt.zh: 海蒂本以为在奶奶家会很难熬，却发现了魔法般的事，也了解到爷爷对内蒂奶奶的爱。回想一次你以为会无聊或糟糕、结果却比想象中好的经历。是什么改变了你的想法？它和海蒂的惊喜有什么相似或不同？

en: Hattie thought two weeks at Grandma's 'might as well be two years' but was 'glad I had' gone (p. 3, 11).
The magical hat let her meet her grandfather, who left a photo with the note 'I love you, Nettie' (p. 22).
A good answer tells a real personal story of a changed expectation and connects it to Hattie's surprise.
zh: 海蒂觉得去奶奶家两周『简直像两年』，但回来时却『很高兴自己去了』（第3、11页）。
那顶有魔力的帽子让她遇见了爷爷，爷爷留下一张照片，背面写着『我爱你，内蒂』（第22页）。
好的回答要讲一个真实的、预期被改变的个人故事，并把它和海蒂的惊喜联系起来。
